title: Integration of qPCR in National Halal Assurance Systems
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The   integration     of     qPCR     into     national   halal   assurance     systems     offers     a     rapid  ,   reliable  ,   and     science-based     approach     to     verifying     the     authenticity     of   halal   food     products  . By   targeting     species-specific   DNA,   qPCR     allows     for     the     detection     of   non-halal   adulterants     such   as   pork     or   non-  slaughtered     animal     content  ,   even   in   highly     processed     products  .   Its     high     sensitivity     and     specificity     enable     authorities     to     strengthen     compliance     monitoring  ,   minimize     fraud  ,   and     enhance     consumer     trust   in halal   certification  .   Adoption     of     qPCR     also     facilitates     harmonization     of   halal   standards     with     international     requirements  ,   supporting   global   trade  .   Government     investment   in   infrastructure  ,   training  ,   and     regulatory     alignment     is     essential     to     fully     leverage     this     technology   as   a     critical     component     of   halal   assurance     systems     and     ensure     consistent     implementation     nationwide  .
